title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text:
Cultural heritage tourism has become increasingly popular (0). recent years as travellers seek authentic experiences. Rather than simply visiting famous landmarks, tourists now want to (1). themselves in local traditions and customs. This shift has created both opportunities and challenges for historic destinations around the world.

Many communities have (2). advantage of this trend by developing programmes that showcase their unique heritage. Local guides offer walking tours that (3). visitors through historic neighbourhoods, sharing stories passed down through generations. Traditional crafts and festivals have been revived to (4). to curious travellers.

However, the growing number of visitors can also (5). a threat to the very places they come to admire. Overcrowding and inappropriate behaviour can damage fragile sites. To (6). with these issues, some destinations have introduced visitor limits and strict regulations. Finding the right (7). between welcoming tourists and protecting heritage requires careful planning. When managed responsibly, cultural tourism can (8). economic benefits while helping preserve traditions for future generations.

example.explanation: 'In recent years' is the correct temporal expression.

questions.explanation: 'Immerse oneself in' significa sumergirse completamente en algo.
'Take advantage of' is the fixed expression for taking advantage of something.
'Lead through' means to guide someone through a place.
'Appeal to' significa atraer o resultar atractivo para alguien.
'Pose a threat' is the correct placement to represent a threat.
'Deal with' is the phrasal verb for handling or facing problems.
'Find the right balance' significa encontrar el equilibrio adecuado.
'Bring benefits' is the correct placement to bring benefits.
